name: Anthology (Blackboard) Education Platform API
what_it_does: Anthology (Blackboard) REST APIs for higher education LMS, student success, and campus management platforms. Enables AI agents to manage course content, enrollment, and learning activity data retrieval for LMS workflow automation, handle Blackboard Learn course and grade management for academic administration, access Blackboard Ally accessibility checking and content enhancement for course quality, retrieve Blackboard Analytics and Anthology Intelligence student engagement data for early alert and student success, handle Anthology Student (SIS) enrollment, financial aid, and student record management, access Anthology Reach CRM data for student recruitment and retention management, retrieve Anthology Finance financial management data for higher education accounting, manage campus commerce and payment data for student accounts, handle alumni engagement and advancement data for development operations, and integrate campus management data with SIS, ERP, and institutional research platforms.
use_cases: Monitoring student engagement from early alert intervention agents
Managing course content from academic administration agents
Tracking enrollment from student information system agents
Automating financial aid processing from student services agents
not_for: K-12 without higher education campus management context
Corporate training without institutional higher education workflows
Non-accredited programs without FERPA and Title IV compliance context
best_when: A higher education institution using Blackboard Learn, Anthology Student, or other Anthology campus management products wants AI agents to automate course management, student success interventions, enrollment processing, financial aid workflow, and institutional analytics.
avoid_when: FERPA RISK: Anthology manages student education records — automated student data access and sharing must comply with FERPA (legitimate educational interest, consent requirements). Student financial aid data is subject to FERPA and Title IV federal student aid regulations; automated financial aid award decisions require financial aid officer review. Early alert and academic standing automation without counselor review creates student due process concerns.

auth.notes: Anthology uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes (3-legged for user-context, 2-legged for system). Developer portal at developer.anthology.com. Boca Raton, Florida HQ. Private equity (Veritas Capital acquired Blackboard 2021; Anthology created via merger of Blackboard + Anthology). Blackboard Learn is the LMS product; Anthology includes campus management, SIS, and student success platforms. Webhooks for Learn events. Competes with Canvas (Instructure) and D2L Brightspace for LMS market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — institutional subscription required
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per product and institution size
pricing.notes: Boca Raton, Florida. Anthology (formed 2021 via Blackboard + Anthology merger, Veritas Capital). Blackboard acquired by Veritas Capital 2021. Anthology absorbs multiple higher education software companies. Blackboard Learn: 5,000+ institutions. Full campus management: LMS, SIS, CRM, finance, and analytics. Competes with Canvas (Instructure), D2L, and Ellucian for higher education market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; documented per product

known_agent_gotchas: FERPA student data compliance — Blackboard Learn and Anthology Student data is FERPA-protected; automated student data access requires legitimate educational interest and proper institutional governance
Post-merger platform complexity — Anthology formed via Blackboard + Anthology merger 2021; multiple product lines (Learn, Student, Reach, Finance, Ally) have separate API surfaces and documentation
Veritas Capital ownership — private equity ownership creates roadmap uncertainty; verify product investment direction for long-term integration decisions
Blackboard Learn SaaS vs. on-premise — Blackboard Learn has both SaaS and self-hosted deployments; API capabilities and version may differ; verify deployment type
Title IV financial aid compliance — Anthology Student financial aid data is subject to federal Title IV regulations; automated financial aid award actions require financial aid officer authorization
Early alert data sensitivity — student academic standing and early alert data is sensitive; automated outreach based on early alert flags must comply with student privacy and due process
agent_readiness.security_notes: Higher education campus management. SOC2, FERPA, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU/AU. Student education and campus records.

security_score_components.security_notes: Higher education LMS and SIS. SOC2, FERPA, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU. Student and campus records.
